summary: PR 2480 linked the Mr. Game & Watch parachute item object by switching itgamewatchparachute.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, adding the item state table data symbol, and reordering two existing logic callback functions in src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c. The only bot report says this produced 1 new match for main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data, moving that unit's .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Build configuration changed itgamewatchparachute.c from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk changes Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c")

key_files.role: Item source updated with ItemStateTable data and function ordering changes needed for the linked/matched object.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds ItemStateTable it_803F78F8[] with two entries using itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim
raw_diff: moves itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_Destroyed earlier in the file
raw_diff: moves itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_EvtUnk to the end of the file

what_changed.text: Marked melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c as Matching in configure.py.
Added global ItemStateTable it_803F78F8 with two states, both using itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim and NULL callbacks for the other slots.
Reordered callback definitions: itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_Destroyed was moved above it_802C6D6C, and itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_EvtUnk was moved to after itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim.

Bot report recorded one new match for main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data, from 0.00% before to 100.00% after, with linked code +776 bytes, matched data +32 bytes, and linked data +40 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Kept the configure.py Matching flip paired with localized source/data changes in the same item translation unit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: only configure.py and src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c changed

smart_moves.text: Used existing callback functions in the new ItemStateTable rather than introducing new wrapper logic.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item objects, a missing ItemStateTable can be the blocker for a .data unit match; adding the state table with existing callback references may be enough to link the object as Matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report says main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data moved from 0.00% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Function definition order can matter for data/link matching when callbacks and table references are involved, but this PR only shows a file-local reorder and does not explain the exact required ordering rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_Destroyed and itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_EvtUnk are moved without changing their bodies

naming_conventions.convention: The PR preserves existing mixed naming in this item file: semantic item callbacks use itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_* or itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim, while unresolved/address-style functions and data keep it_802C*/it_803F* names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802C6C38, it_802C6D6C, it_802C6DB8, it_802C6DD8, it_803F78F8

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch object status in configure.py only after the item file supplies the missing matching data/source structure.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report records 1 new .data match

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use file-local symbol ordering as a matching lever when function bodies are already present but data/link output is not matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: moved two existing functions with unchanged bodies
metadata_json: PR number/title/author/state/timestamps and changed file counts
raw_diff: complete 2603-byte diff loaded and not truncated
changed_files.jsonl: changed files and hunk counts
text_corpus.jsonl: empty PR body and one decomp-dev[bot] report
notes: Diff evidence is strong for changed files and exact source edits.
The PR body is empty, and there are no review comments or reviews, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not directly evidenced.
The bot report supports a new .data match but does not provide build logs, objdiff output, or detailed rationale for why the function reorder was necessary.
No unsupported validation beyond the loaded bot report is claimed.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ItemStateTable additions can unlock item .data matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2480, adding ItemStateTable it_803F78F8[] to src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c and switching the object to Matching coincided with the bot report showing main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data moving from 0.00% to 100.00%. Use this as a candidate lesson for item .data matching, not as a global rule without more examples.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py switches itgamewatchparachute.c to Matching
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report says 1 new match for .data

source_update_candidates.title: itgamewatchparachute.c marked Matching by PR 2480
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2480 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c is listed as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk changes Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c")
source_update_candidates.reason: Path/status fact candidate for curator review; do not treat as current source truth without checking present configure.py.

source_update_candidates.title: Game & Watch parachute item state table symbol
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2480 added ItemStateTable it_803F78F8[] in src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c with two entries referencing itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim.
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol/path fact candidate grounded in the PR diff.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a global requirement that item callback functions must always be ordered this way; this PR only shows a local reorder.
Do not claim a text-section function match for a named function from this slice; the bot report specifically lists the new match as .data.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; the loaded slice has no reviews or review comments.
